agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"POST /validate","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"No circuit breaker protecting the service from SmartyStreets outages. A prolonged SmartyStreets degradation (slow responses just under 3s) will exhaust connection pools and cascade to callers."},{"from":"/health","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Health check couples liveness to an external dependency. If SmartyStreets is down, orchestrators (K8s) will restart healthy pods, causing unnecessary churn. Split into /healthz (liveness, always 200) and /readyz (readiness, checks SmartyStreets)."}],"contract_compliance":"No input validation contract visible — unclear what happens with malformed or empty address payloads. Callers have no schema to code against.","data_flow_integrity":"Cannot verify data flow integrity in production because neither the input address nor the raw SmartyStreets response is logged. A mismatch between what we sent and what we got back is invisible."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No request ID / correlation ID in any log line — impossible to trace a single request through distributed logs","No duration logged for SmartyStreets API call — cannot identify slow calls from logs alone","Input address not logged (even redacted) — cannot reproduce or debug a specific failed validation","Raw SmartyStreets response not logged (even at DEBUG level) — cannot determine if our parsing is wrong vs. upstream returned bad data","No structured logging apparent — grep-based debugging is fragile at scale"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Minimal. A single counter exists with no dimensional breakdown.","gaps":["validation_requests_total lacks a result label (valid/invalid/error) — cannot alert on validation error rate spikes","No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds) — cannot set SLO on p99 latency or detect SmartyStreets degradation","No metric for SmartyStreets errors/timeouts specifically — cannot distinguish our bugs from upstream failures","No in-flight request gauge — cannot detect connection pool exhaustion"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry / Jaeger spans) — in a microservice architecture, this request is a black box to upstream callers","No span for the outbound SmartyStreets HTTP call — cannot attribute latency to network vs. processing"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am seeing elevated error rates has no way to: (1) find a specific failing request, (2) see what address was sent, (3) see what SmartyStreets returned, or (4) measure how long the call took. They can only see that requests happened and whether they were valid/invalid."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns HTTP 500 or is completely down","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Return a clear 503 to callers with a Retry-After header. Implement a circuit breaker (e.g. 5 failures in 30s → open for 60s) so the service fails fast instead of burning through the 3s timeout on every request."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets is slow (2.5s responses, just under timeout)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"This will look 'healthy' but destroy caller latency. Add a latency histogram + alert when p99 exceeds 1s. Consider an adaptive timeout or deadline propagation from the caller."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets rate-limits the service (HTTP 429)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Detect 429 responses, log them distinctly, expose a metric, and implement exponential backoff or a token-bucket on outbound calls."},{"scenario":"Malformed or empty address input","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate input before making an external call. Return 400 with a clear error message. Do not waste a SmartyStreets API call on garbage input."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets API key expires or is revoked","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Detect 401/403, log at ERROR with a distinct message, expose a metric. This should fire a high-severity alert — it's a total outage, not a transient failure."},{"scenario":"DNS resolution failure for SmartyStreets endpoint","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Will trigger the 3s timeout eventually, but the error message will be opaque. Catch DNS errors explicitly and log/metric them separately."}],"retry_config":"No retry policy mentioned. A single transient 500 from SmartyStreets fails the entire request. Add 1 retry with jitter for 5xx/timeout, but NOT for 4xx.","timeout_config":"3s timeout exists — reasonable starting point, but no deadline propagation from callers and no adaptive behavior.","circuit_breakers":"None. This is the single biggest resilience gap. Prolonged SmartyStreets outage will cause every request to block for 3s before failing.","graceful_degradation":"None. Consider: when SmartyStreets is unavailable, return a degraded response (e.g. {\"status\": \"unverified\", \"reason\": \"upstream_unavailable\"}) instead of a hard 500, letting callers decide whether to accept unverified addresses."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Acceptable for a stateless service with no database migrations. Verify that SmartyStreets API key and config are not baked into the image (should be env vars / secrets).","monitoring_coverage":"Insufficient. Cannot alert on error rate by type, cannot set latency SLOs, cannot detect upstream degradation vs. internal bugs.","on_call_documentation":"No runbook mentioned. On-call engineer needs at minimum: (1) what this service does, (2) what SmartyStreets is and its status page URL, (3) what to do when it's down, (4) how to verify the API key, (5) how to read the logs.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add Prometheus latency histogram metric","Add result label to counter metric","Add structured logging with request IDs"],"infra_provisioning":["Alerting rules for error rate, latency p99, and circuit breaker state","Grafana dashboard for the service","Optional: OpenTelemetry collector if not already deployed"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Stateless service, no database involved."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No request correlation IDs, no latency histogram, no input/response logging, no distributed tracing","reason":"An on-call engineer cannot debug any production issue with current observability. Cannot determine WHAT failed, WHY it failed, or HOW LONG it took. This is a blind deployment.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No circuit breaker, no retry policy, no graceful degradation","reason":"A SmartyStreets outage will cascade into this service blocking all requests for 3s each, exhausting resources and appearing healthy to the load balancer. No fallback behavior exists.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Health check design couples liveness to external dependency","reason":"Kubernetes will restart pods during a SmartyStreets outage because liveness probe fails, causing unnecessary churn and log loss. Must separate liveness from readiness probes.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability gaps are clear and well-understood","Resilience gaps are standard patterns with known solutions","Health check anti-pattern is a well-documented Kubernetes concern"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot verify input validation behavior without seeing code","Cannot confirm whether structured logging framework is in use","Unknown whether callers have their own timeout/retry logic that would compound"],"assumptions":["Service is deployed on Kubernetes or similar orchestrator","SmartyStreets is the only external dependency","No database or persistent state","The service is not behind an API gateway that provides its own circuit breaking"]}}
